spec_match.rationale: Calls navigator.credentials.create({ digital }) — the v143 addition — and uses the openid4vci protocol with credential_offer_uri or doctype/format payload. Matches the w3c-fedid digital-credentials shape.

interactive.rationale: Probe panel, dropdown for format / doctype / protocol, live request preview, Issue button, clear button. Real API invocation.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Index promises 'Build an issuance request live and call navigator.credentials.create from the page. Feature probes show what the browser exposes and report the outcome.' Delivered.

edge_cases.rationale: Handles missing credentials.create, surfaces absent-wallet errors, and now has a visible flag-required banner. It still does not validate the request schema or explain the desktop CTAP handoff.

references.rationale: See-also includes https://chromestatus.com/feature/5099333963874304 and the w3c-fedid spec.

openQuestions.title: Show the request body that's about to be sent in JSON-schema-validated form
openQuestions.detail: The page builds JSON live but never validates against the OpenID4VCI request schema. Adding a small inline validation step (with helpful errors when, e.g., `credential_offer_uri` is missing for openid4vci) would prevent a NotSupportedError that's actually a malformed-request issue.

openQuestions.title: Document the cross-device CTAP flow on desktop
openQuestions.detail: ChromeStatus motivation explicitly mentions: 'On Desktop, it leverages cross-device approaches using the CTAP protocol'. The page never mentions desktop being different. A small 'this is what happens on desktop' callout (QR-code-to-phone handoff) would teach the platform behaviour.

openQuestions.title: Probe `DigitalCredential` instance shape after issuance
openQuestions.detail: If the API call resolves with a credential, the page only logs type + id. Surfacing the `data` payload (truncated for length) would let visitors inspect the actual issued credential bytes — useful for debugging.

summary: Solid live-API demo for the v143 issuance addition. The page now has a readable flag-required banner; schema validation, desktop CTAP context, and richer successful credential logging remain useful follow-ups.
